context.projectOverview: Ports the Claude Agent SDK agent-loop capabilities into agent-bober's own provider-agnostic loop as additive, default-off changes. Sprints 1-4 implement the four architecture-backed high-value gaps (refusal detection, cost substrate + USD budget ceiling, per-role effort, parallel read-only tools); sprints 5-10 implement the remaining capability areas (loop events + hooks, sessions/resume/fork, in-context compaction, streaming deltas, mid-turn interrupt, in-process subagents + opt-in MCP bridge) in the own-loop idiom per explicit user direction, bound by the same hard constraints.

completedSprints.title: Refusal detection end-to-end
completedSprints.summary: Commit 35a2dbd: explicit 'refusal' StopReason case in anthropic+openai adapters (openai also maps message.refusal + content_filter), spread-conditional refused:true on AgenticLoopResult completion branch (never throws), parseGeneratorResult exported with fail-closed refusal guard before the filesWritten shortcut. Suite 3686->3699. Non-refusal path byte-identical.

contract.title: Cost substrate: CostMeter price table, ChatResponse.costUsd, Budget maxUsd
contract.description: Build the money layer with no loop changes. A new pure CostMeter maps (provider, model, tokenUsage) to a USD estimate via a static all-provider price table (longest-prefix match, unknown → undefined). Every adapter populates optional ChatResponse.costUsd — claude-code returns its real, currently-discarded total_cost_usd and never estimates (ADR-3); anthropic/openai/openai-compat (and google where usage is normalized) use the CostMeter. Budget gains an optional maxUsd ceiling alongside tokens/agents.

successCriteria.description: estimateCostUsd({provider, model, usage}) returns the correct USD for known models via longest-prefix match on `${provider}:${modelPrefix}` keys and undefined for unknown models; the table carries rows for anthropic, openai, openai-compat (DeepSeek, xAI/Grok) and google with an as-priced date comment.
The claude-code adapter returns costUsd equal to the CLI's parsed total_cost_usd (vendor-authoritative), never a CostMeter estimate, and omits the field when the CLI output lacks it.
The anthropic and openai/openai-compat adapters set costUsd from estimateCostUsd when a price row matches and omit the field entirely when it returns undefined.

Budget accepts maxUsd (null/omitted = unlimited): chargeUsd accumulates into usdSpent; remainingUsd() returns Infinity when uncapped; exceeded() and assertWithinBudget() honor the USD axis with BudgetExceededError kind 'usd'; NaN/negative/undefined charges are treated as 0.
All pre-existing Budget token/agent tests and provider adapter tests pass unchanged (byte-identical when maxUsd/costUsd are absent).
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass.

nonGoals: Do not wire Budget into the agentic loop or any role (sprint 3).
Do not add config schema fields (sprint 3).
Do not make the price table config-overridable (explicitly declined in planning Q1).
Do not fetch prices at runtime — the table is static and in-repo.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: prefix matching + undefined for unknown, claude-code real-cost passthrough, adapter estimate-or-omit behavior, and the full USD Budget axis including error kind 'usd'.
contract.definitionOfDone: A pure src/providers/cost-meter.ts exposes estimateCostUsd with a dated all-provider PriceTable; every API adapter populates optional ChatResponse.costUsd (real for claude-code); Budget enforces an optional maxUsd with chargeUsd/usdSpent/remainingUsd and BudgetExceededError kind 'usd'. Nothing consumes these yet; omitting them is byte-identical. Build and full suite green.
assumptions: claude-code.ts already parses total_cost_usd (~:55) and discards it in the return block (~:182-190); this sprint threads it into the return.
ChatResponse (src/providers/types.ts:212-224) gains optional costUsd — LLMClient.chat signature unchanged.
Budget lives at src/orchestrator/workflow/budget.ts (BudgetOptions ~:19-24, BudgetExceededError ~:27-36); its existing workflow-interpreter callers must remain source-compatible.

ProviderName union comes from src/providers/factory.ts (~:13); DeepSeek/Grok run through openai-compat so their price keys use model-prefix distinctions (e.g. 'openai-compat:deepseek', 'openai-compat:grok').
Price values are best-known list prices at build time with an as-priced date comment; guardrail-not-billing semantics (approved planning answer Q1).
contract.generatorNotes: Create src/providers/cost-meter.ts: export type PriceRow { inputPerMillion: number; outputPerMillion: number }, type PriceTable = Record<string, PriceRow> keyed `${provider}:${modelPrefix}`, and estimateCostUsd(input: {provider, model, usage}): number | undefined implementing longest-prefix match (sort matching keys by prefix length desc). Include a '// Prices as of 2026-07 (list prices; guardrail semantics, not billing)' comment. Populate rows for anthropic (claude-fable-5, claude-opus-4, claude-sonnet-5, claude-haiku-4-5 prefixes), openai, openai-compat:deepseek, openai-compat:grok, google:gemini — verify current list prices before hardcoding; if a price cannot be confirmed, omit the row (undefined is the safe fail-open) rather than guessing. Return undefined for provider 'claude-code' unconditionally. Extend budget.ts additively: maxUsd on BudgetOptions, chargeUsd (guard !Number.isFinite(usd) || usd < 0 → 0), usdSpent getter, remainingUsd() (Infinity when uncapped), extend exceeded()/assertWithinBudget(), add 'usd' to the kind union. In each adapter, compute costUsd right where the normalized ChatResponse is assembled (anthropic ~:348 return, openai usage normalize ~:455-457, openai-compat mirrors openai, claude-code return block ~:182-190) using conditional spread so the key is absent when undefined. Do NOT import cost-meter in claude-code.ts.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Test estimateCostUsd: exact prefix, longer-prefix-wins (e.g. 'anthropic:claude-sonnet' vs 'anthropic:claude'), unknown model → undefined, claude-code → undefined, arithmetic correctness (tokens/1e6 * price summed for input+output) (sc-2-1). Mock the claude CLI JSON with and without total_cost_usd and assert passthrough/omission (sc-2-2). For adapters, stub the SDK response and deep-assert the returned ChatResponse: costUsd present with expected value for a priced model, key ABSENT (Object.hasOwn false) for an unpriced one (sc-2-3). Budget: charge sequences crossing the ceiling, remainingUsd Infinity uncapped, assertWithinBudget throws BudgetExceededError with kind 'usd', NaN/negative → 0 (sc-2-4). Run the FULL suite for sc-2-5 and build/typecheck for sc-2-6.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
